tender.title: Temporary Accommodation for Staff
tender.description:
Central London Community Healthcare NHS Trust are looking to establish a framework for temporary accommodation to support new staff from either the UK or recruited internationally. Accommodation is required to between 1-5 rooms and available for a 1-2 month period, with an option for staff to continue their tenancy after this period. This opportunity has 3 lots covering: North London, South London and Hertfordshire. Bidders may submit a bid for as many lots as they feel able.
Three suppliers will be appointed to each lot, based on their total score at the end of the evaluation. Each lot will be tiered on a first right of refusal basis, with the highest scoring supplier having the first right of refusal.

tender.title: Accommodation-based Housing-related Support Service for Homeless Families and Adults
tender.description: The Accommodation-based Housing-related Support Service for Homeless Families and Adults provided under this contract is to deliver housing-related support to assist service users to access and sustain independent living. The Service is designed to enable families and adults to transition from supported or temporary accommodation to independence and to maintain suitable, sustainable and settled accommodation.

tender.title: GB-St Helens: Provision of 'person centred' IT training for tenants in sheltered accommodation
tender.description:
Torus Foundation is seeking to appoint a single service provider to enter into a Contract for the provision of 'person centred' IT training for tenants in sheltered accommodation.
      
      The scope of the contract is to:
      • Engage tenants aged 55 and over who live in sheltered and extra care accommodation in a 'person centred' and flexible IT training programme. The training should be practical and delivered in a very accessible way. The focus should be on what you can do with IT and the digital world rather than the technical aspects of IT.
      • The training sessions should be enjoyable and engaging with many practical examples and applications. Training should aim to improve the efficacy of this group of tenants with regard to using the digital world.
      • Carry out an IT skills confidence and needs assessments with tenants.
      • Liaise with Torus Foundation and the Torus IT team to gain a thorough understanding of the IT infrastructure within the accommodation.
      • Design an IT skills programme designed to help tenants gain entry level skills with the facility to enable tenants to progress onto further training. This training must be flexible to allow for tenants to get the skills and confidence that they need. A structured rigid programme may not suit older tenants.
      • Recruit, train and retain volunteer digital champions in each sheltered scheme to support IT 
      learning and the ongoing experience of the digital world.

Additional information: The contracting authority considers that this contract may be suitable for economic operators that are small or medium enterprises (SMEs). However, any selection of tenderers will be based solely on the criteria set out for the procurement.

tender.title: Housing Related Support for Vulnerable Single People, Couples, and Families
tender.description:
North East Lincolnshire Council (the Authority) is undertaking a procurement for a Housing Related Support (HRS) Framework for vulnerable single people, couples and families.

The Framework is split in to two lots:

Lot 1 - High Needs
Lot 2 - Moderate Needs

Tenderers can choose to submit a bid for one or both lots.

The Framework will be awarded to a maximum of eight (8) Tenderers for Lot 1, and a maximum of eight (8) Tenderers for Lot 2. This will be based on the award criteria stated in the Invitation to Tender documents.

The Framework aims to deliver flexible HRS and bespoke, outcome-focused support packages to service users who live and/or work in the North East Lincolnshire Borough. The service will be available to vulnerable single people, couples and families and expectant mothers over the age of 16 requiring HRS to access accommodation or maintain their accommodation independently.

This procurement is being run under the Light Touch Regime using a Light Touch Open Process.

The Framework has an initial duration of 36 months with the option to extend for 2 further 12 month periods. The Framework has an additional year to allow a transition from the old service delivery to the new service delivery.

All documents are only available via www.yortender.co.uk

All clarifications should be submitted via www.yortender.co.uk

Additional information: Please use the following link to access the opportunity https://procontract.due-north.com/Advert?advertId=55009000-a58c-eb11-810c-005056b64545&fromProjectDashboard=True

tender.title: DOMESTIC ABUSE - SAFE SUPPORTED ACCOMMODATION SERVICE
tender.description:
The Safe Zones service will provide emergency, safe accommodation and whole family support for those who have suffered Domestic Abuse, within the last 6 months, or who are at risk of it.
  
The service sits within commissioned pathways for supported housing and domestic abuse services in Sheffield and it will collaborate closely with other domestic abuse services.
